title: Protohopea tinctoria
intro: Protohopea tinctoria, also known as yellow copaiba, is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Protohopea tinctoria include lignans, terpenes, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include essential oils, extracts, and infusions, which are used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
taxonomy: Protohopea tinctoria is classified within the family Anacardiaceae, which includes other plants like cashews and poison ivy.
